cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So, Jane thinks today is 6/18/2019, but John says it's 6/19/2019. And the problem tells us that John is correct. So today's date is 6/19/2019. The question is asking for the date one year ago from today in the MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, and I need to pick the correct one. First, I need to calculate one year before 6/19/2019. If I subtract a year from 2019, that would be 2018. So the year part would be 2018. Then the month and day would stay the same, right? Because if today is June 19, 2019, then one year ago would be June 19, 2018. Wait, is there any catch here? Hmm, maybe not. Unless there's some problem with leap years or something. Let me think. 2019 is not a leap year. 2018 isn't either. So February had 28 days in both years. So if you subtract exactly one year, the date should be the same except for the year. So June 19, 2018. Let me check the options. Looking at the options: (A) 06/19/2018 (B) 05/22/2018 (C) 06/19/1934 (D) 06/19/1956 (E) 06/24/2018 (F) 06/18/2018 So option A is 06/19/2018, which matches my calculation.
